_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in creating career path definitions
reference_document_element_description: Conduct data and AI workforce planning, including current-state skills assessment, future-state requirements modeling, gap analysis, and talent acquisition and development strategy formulation.
comments: NICE skill intersects with WIDAI LS domain. Task LS-T-006 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: LS-S-009 (LS, cosine: 0.521), LS-K-009 (LS, cosine: 0.511).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 2.8/10 (raw 0.279). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. NICE-only terms [career, creating, definitions, path, skill] vs WIDAI-only terms [acquisition, ai, analysis, assessment, conduct, current] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.863 (P=0.876, R=0.850). P exceeds R by 0.026 — the NICE element's 6 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 22 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the NICE element.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (91.6%). "Skill in creating career path definitions" and "Conduct data and AI workforce planning, including current-state skills assessmen" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.525 > STS 0.279 (gap +0.246). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 0.0%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. NICE terms: [career, creating, definitions, path, skill]. WIDAI terms: [acquisition, ai, analysis, assessment, conduct, current].

signals.detail: Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.246 with low STS 0.279. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
